Kenon is currently trading on a P/E of 69x, which is high relative to many markets, and sits against a last close of ₪266.8 that already reflects strong recent price gains.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share, so a higher figure usually means investors are willing to pay more today for each unit of current profit. For a power generation and energy company like Kenon, that can sometimes reflect expectations around future projects, contract visibility, or shifts in the business mix across natural gas and renewables.
